articles.title: Horoscope for Sunday, June 21, 2026
articles.description:
Moon Alert

Avoid shopping (except food and gas) and important decisions from noon to 4:30 p.m. After that, the moon moves from Virgo into Libra.

Aries (March 21-April 19)

An average day

For the next four weeks, your focus will be on home, family and your private life more than usual. Family discussions and home repairs are likely. You'll express your ideas with conviction. You'll also enjoy playful times with kids, sports and the arts.

Taurus (April 20-May 20)

A positive day

The pace of your days will accelerate in the next four weeks because you have places to go, things to do and people to see. Conversations with others will be lively. In addition, home redecorating as well as entertaining will appeal to you.

Gemini (May 21-June 20)

An average day

With fiery Mars in your sign, you have energy to burn. Meanwhile, in the next four weeks, you will focus on earnings, cash flow, money, as well as purchases. Communications with others are sweet and charming now. Appreciate the beauty of your daily surroundings.

Cancer (June 21-July 22)

A positive day

Today the sun moves into your sign to stay for the next four weeks. This can happen only once a year, and when it occurs, it makes you project yourself onto the world around you with greater intensity. This is also your chance to recharge your batteries for the rest of the year.

Leo (July 23-Aug. 22)

An average day

Your personal year is coming to an end, which is why the next four weeks, before the sun enters your sign, will be a more private time for you. Think of goals that you want to achieve in the coming year. How do you want your new year to be different from this last year?

Virgo (Aug. 23-Sept. 22)

A positive day

Your popularity will increase in the next four weeks. Seek out old friends and make new ones. Explore the possibility of joining clubs, groups and associations. Your involvement with younger people will be stronger.

Libra (Sept. 23-Oct. 22)

An average day

Once a year, for four weeks, the sun casts you in a spotlight and this light is flattering! Everyone will admire you even if you don't do anything different. This gives you an advantage that starts today! Go after what you want.

Scorpio (Oct. 23-Nov. 21)

A positive day

Grab every opportunity to travel or do something to expand your world in the next four weeks because you will have a chance to do this. Opportunities will also exist in publishing, the media, medicine, the law and higher education. You rock!

Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)

An average day

Despite your desire to travel for pleasure, you will find that in the next four weeks, your focus on shared property, taxes, debt, insurance issues, inheritances and the wealth of partners will be stronger. Be patient with those who are closest to you while Mars is opposite your sign.

Capricorn (Dec. 22-Jan. 19)

A positive day

You will need more sleep in the next four weeks because the sun is now opposing your sign, which will drain your energy somewhat. Nevertheless, your focus on partners and close friends will be strong. Money might come to you. Romance will be affectionate.

Aquarius (Jan. 20-Feb. 18)

An average day

Roll up your sleeves because you're going to be busy and productive in the next four weeks. Not only will you work harder than usual, but you will be keen to be as efficient and effective as possible. You might also want to improve your health. Oh yes, you want it all!

Pisces (Feb. 19-March 20)

A positive day

Lucky you! The next four weeks promise fun and games. Enjoy vacations, social outings, sports events, playful activities with kids and greater opportunities to express your creative talents.

If today is your birthday

Prince William (1982) shares your birthday today. You're adventurous and enjoy the company of others. You're friendly, empathetic and you love the arts. This year is one of fresh beginnings and major changes in your life. Keep your eyes open for new opportunities and be ready to take action. What you do now will bring great benefits in the future.

articles.title: Paths carved into boreal peatlands for oil and gas exploration could be speeding up climate change: study
articles.description: Thousands of paths, known as seismic lines, carved into Canada's boreal peatlands for oil and gas exploration could be speeding up climate change, suggests a study from University of Waterloo researchers. In Alberta's boreal peatlands alone, these paths, found in B.C. and Saskatchewan as well, are long enough to wrap around the Earth nine times.

articles.title: From coal to cabernet: the wine seller using a flooded mine to cut heating bills
articles.description: Lanchester Wines in north-east England uses heat from a disused coalmine to maintain wine temperatures and with 23,000 flooded mines in the UK, there’s huge potential for more businesses and homes to follow its lead Shove them in a fridge, stash them in a cellar – this is how most people store their favourite bottles of wine. But if you have warehouses full of thousands of vintages, you have to think a little differently. For the last eight winters, Lanchester Wines has used heat from a disused coalmine to maintain ideal storage temperatures at its facilities in the north-east of England, helping to prevent freezing or spoilage. Continue reading...
